string.text: Maximum number of bandit militia heroes per bandit clan kept alive in the recycle pool. Released, escaped, and imprisoned BM heroes are reused up to this cap; killed/executed heroes are removed permanently and new heroes are being created.
Bandit parties smaller than this will not merge into a Bandit Militia.
How likely every day Bandit Militias is to split when large enough.
Bandit heroes will train their militias.
Each day they might train further.
Hardest grants enough XP to significantly upgrade troops. Off grants no bonus XP.
At most this percentage of troops will be upgraded each time training occurs. Looters are included.
Chance per day that the militia will gain more troops (0 for off).
Grow each troop type by this percent.
Training will never upgrade troops beyond this tier.
They won't be attacked by BMs.
BMs will not engage parties stronger than themselves by more than this %. 100 means attack regardless of how strong the target is.
10 means any party 10% weaker will be ignored.\n100 attacks without restriction.
The chance of Bandit Militias AI to consider raiding a village. It triggers once per in-game hour for every bandit militia party, so a smaller value is advised.
Scales the maximum number of simultaneous village raids. The baseline is auto-calculated as (total villages ÷ 10). 100% = default. Lower = fewer raids at once, Higher = more raids at once.
What to name a Bandit Militia.
What to name a Bandit Militia with no leader.
Shows all Bandit Militias on the map.
Any smaller BMs won't be tracked.
Bandit Militias will have unique banners, or the default bandit clan banners.
When your fiefs are raided you'll see a banner message.
Remove the messages of Bandit Militia Heroes being taken or released as prisoners.

Double-check whether the bandit voice lines match the leader's gender. Some official voice lines use male voices but do not specify gender, so female bandit leaders will speak with male voices if this option is disabled.
The log file is at "Documents/Mount and Blade II Bannerlord/Configs/ModLogs/BanditMilitia_[Time].log"
{LEADER.FIRSTNAME} is a brigand chief leading a band of cutthroats that raids under the name of the {CLAN_NAME}.
Across the land {?LEADER.GENDER}she{?}he{\\?} is spoken of as {LEADER.FIRSTNAME} {EPITHET}, victor of {WINS} {?MANY}battles{?}battle{\\?}.
Across the land {?LEADER.GENDER}she{?}he{\\?} is spoken of as {LEADER.FIRSTNAME} {EPITHET}.
Who are you, and who do you ride for?
They call me {LEADER_NAME}. We answer to no lord and no king — only to coin and the open road.
I see. Now, back to business.
Surrender now and I might let you live.
Ha! You'll have to take our heads first. At them, lads!
You collected a bounty of {GOLD} denars and {RENOWN} renown for {LEADER}.
Write diagnostic messages to the in-game chat. Off by default; enable only when troubleshooting.
Write diagnostic messages to Modules\BanditMilitiasRedux\Logs\BMR_*.log. Off by default; enable only when troubleshooting.
Skip conversations when encountering Bandit Militias. Bribery will be unavailable if enabled.
Limit the per-piece value of equipment given to the Heroes.\nMostly for when other mods give you Hero loot.
No BMs smaller than this will form.
How many looters get made into better units when training.
Caps the total combat strength of all Bandit Militias combined as a percentage of all other parties in the world. Higher values allow more and stronger BMs but can noticeably impact world balance.
A Bandit Militia cannot merge or split again until this many in-game hours have passed.
Bandit Militias move at full speed regardless of party size.
{raidedSettlement?.Name} raided! {party.Name} is fat with loot near {townName}!
Chance for a Bandit Militia leader to be female. 0 = all male, 100 = all female.
